item.title: Contact loss in all-solid-state Li-ion batteries via deposition of impurities
item.description: Interfacial contact loss in solid-state batteries is often attributed to the formation of voids in pristine lithium (Li) metal at the Li-solid electrolyte interface. We show that insulating impurities within the Li anode accumulate at the interface during stripping and create a porous, mechanically stable layer that blocks the flux of Li into the electrolyte.

item.title: Learning battery aging from early electrochemical signals
item.description: Reliable prognosis of long-term battery degradation remains a critical challenge. In a recent issue of Cell Reports Physical Science, Prakash et al. address this challenge using early electrochemical signals and data-driven modeling. They extract physics-informed features and integrate them with machine learning, enabling accurate lifetime and aging prediction.

item.title: Catalyzed anionic redox in sulfide electrolytes for high-energy all-solid-state organic batteries
item.description:
Publication date: 15 April 2026

Source: Joule, Volume 10, Issue 4

Author(s): Qihang Yu, Yang Hu, Sixu Deng, Mohsen Shakouri, Graham King, Lo-Yueh Chang, Karim Zaghib, Tsun-Kong Sham, Xia Li

item.title: Taming Active Material-Solid Electrolyte Interfaces with Organic Cathode for All-Solid-State Batteries

Publication date: 20 May 2026

Source: Joule, Volume 10, Issue 5

Author(s): Fang Hao, Xiaowei Chi, Yanliang Liang, Ye Zhang, Rong Xu, Hua Guo, Tanguy Terlier, Hui Dong, Kejie Zhao, Jun Lou, Yan Yao

item.title: Disentangling ion migration from artifacts in high-fidelity ToF-SIMS depth profiling of perovskite solar cells
item.description:
Publication date: Available online 28 May 2026

Source: Joule

Author(s): Nico Fransaert, Jean V. Manca, Shabnam Ahadzadeh, Bart Ruttens, Jan D’Haen, Dirk Valkenborg, Bart Cleuren, Bart Vermang, Aslihan H. Babayigit
